{"step_by_step_thinking": "Efgartigimod is a neonatal Fc receptor (FcRn) antagonist that reduces the half-life of pathogenic IgG antibodies by enhancing their degradation. Multiple documents confirm that efgartigimod is being used or tested in myasthenia gravis. Document [1] mentions that Fc receptor antagonists such as efgartigimod represent novel targeted therapies for severe cases of myasthenia gravis. Document [7] explicitly states that efgartigimod and rozanolixizumab are FcRn antagonists that lower pathogenic antibodies and are approved for use in myasthenia gravis. Document [17] and [23] further confirm that efgartigimod is undergoing testing for use in myasthenia gravis and has shown response rates in clinical studies. Although some documents note that eculizumab and rituximab are used in refractory cases, efgartigimod is specifically highlighted as a targeted therapy for myasthenia gravis. Therefore, based on the evidence, efgartigimod can be used for myasthenia gravis.", "answer_choice": "A"}
